selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 7.4% and 20-day momentum is 17.8%.
Trend quality is supported by 80% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 9.1% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 1.8%, downside volatility near 1.4%, and a recent max drawdown of 5.2%.
Volume confirmation is 6.5% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 5.9% versus SPY over 5 days and 2.1% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.69, confidence 0.43, and 0.4 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.84, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.4 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.87, average source quality 0.77, confidence 0.79.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC 0.02).
Overall model score is 0.195 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Launches Dual-Energy Cardiac Ablation Catheter in Europe
news_evidence.llm_reason: Announcement of a dual-energy cardiac ablation catheter launch in Europe is a company-specific product development and positive for JNJ's medtech franchise. However, product launches typically have gradual revenue impact and are unlikely to drive large stock moves within 1–10 trading days absent additional catalysts (e.g., major regulatory rulings, sizable contracts, or buy/sell analyst updates tied to the launch).
